fields.description:
## What

Introduce the low-level **bare-ref CAS lock primitive** the reconciler pass-lock and phase-gate
will sit on. Ref -> blob, so it never lives in the tickets working tree and is never union-merged.
No reconciler wiring yet (C3). Also **prove the remote feasibility** the whole design rests on.

- **AC0 feasibility spike (do this first — it gates the rest):** prove a non-branch
  `refs/reconciler/*` ref pointing at a **blob** round-trips (push + fetch) through the `origin`
  GitHub remote using the CI `GITHUB_TOKEN` (contents:write). If GitHub rejects it, fall back to
  ref -> tiny-commit and record that in the ADR. Document the working refspec.
- New module `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` exposing `acquire`/`release`/`read`
  over a configurable ref name (`refs/reconciler/lock`, `refs/reconciler/gate` share one schema).
- **Blob = newline-terminated UTF-8 JSON** `{holder, lease_secs, heartbeat_ns, fence}`, clock from
  `time.time_ns()` (NOT `rebar._store.hlc` — that fcntl cache orders ticket events, wrong tool).
  `heartbeat_ns` is the acquisition/renew anchor; `fence` is a monotonic int (progress witness +
  fencing token). Decode of a corrupt/partial/empty blob **fails closed**: `read` raises a defined
  error and callers treat "cannot parse" as HELD, never free.
- **Blob creation:** `git hash-object -w --stdin` plants the blob and yields the OID, THEN
  `git update-ref <ref> <oid> <old>`: **create-only CAS acquire** uses old = 40 zeros; **release**
  uses old = the exact observed OID.
- **Reuse the real CAS helper** `_cas_advance_with_retry` (`_advisory_lock.py:447-487`), NOT a
  second primitive. Its discriminator `_is_cas_mismatch` (`_advisory_lock.py:415`, hard-coding
  `'refs/heads/tickets'` at line 425) is **generalized to accept the ref name** (or a ref-agnostic
  variant is extracted) so a CAS exit-128 on `refs/reconciler/*` is classified correctly. Promote
  the reused private helper to a shared/semipublic symbol as needed.
- **Acquire vs release retry semantics differ:** a create-only acquire CAS exit-128 means "lock
  already held" — a definitive result, **not retried**. A release CAS exit-128 is a benign race
  (already released) and is treated as success (idempotent release). Steal-CAS (C2) exit-128 is the
  single-winner race — the loser stops, it does not re-acquire.
- No lease-expiry/steal logic here (that is C2); a plain `acquire` fails if the ref already exists.
- Add structured log emission for acquire-failure (already held) and release on a stale oid.
- Record the bare-ref-lock design decision (schema, CAS contract, remote refspec) in a new ADR
  under `docs/adr/` (extended by later children).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC0: a proving command/test demonstrates `refs/reconciler/*` (ref -> blob) push+fetch through
      the `origin` GitHub remote with the CI token; the working refspec (and any ref->commit
      fallback) is documented in the ADR.
- [ ] `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` provides `acquire`/`release`/`read` over a
      configurable ref; acquire is create-only CAS (old=zeros), release is observed-oid CAS; blob is
      created via `git hash-object -w` before `update-ref`.
- [ ] Blob is UTF-8 JSON `{holder, lease_secs, heartbeat_ns, fence}` (`time.time_ns()`); `read` of a
      corrupt/empty blob raises and callers treat it as HELD (fail-closed), never free.
- [ ] The CAS reuses `_cas_advance_with_retry` (`_advisory_lock.py:447-487`); `_is_cas_mismatch`
      (`:415`) is generalized off the hard-coded `refs/heads/tickets` to accept the ref name; acquire
      exit-128 ("held") is NOT retried while release exit-128 is treated as idempotent success.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: acquire on a free ref; second concurrent acquire fails (already exists);
      `read` on present vs absent ref (defined contract); release requires the correct old oid and a
      stale-oid release is rejected; corrupt-blob decode fails closed.
- [ ] The bare-ref-lock decision is captured in a new `docs/adr/` entry. Non-integration suite green.
